senses.definition: the permission given to someone to become a student of a school, enter an organization, etc.

examples.x: She received her letter of admission to the university, confirming her acceptance into the engineering program.
Admission to the club requires filling out an application and attending an interview with the board members.
He was thrilled to receive admission to his top-choice graduate school after months of hard work on his application.
The museum offers free admission to children under the age of twelve.
Admission to the concert is included with the purchase of a festival pass.

senses.definition: the state of being present at an event or a place

examples.x: The attendance at the conference exceeded expectations, with over 500 participants from around the world.
She takes attendance at the beginning of each class to track students' participation.
The theater was filled to capacity, with every seat occupied, indicating strong attendance for the performance.
The church saw a decline in attendance after implementing a new schedule for services.
The company encourages regular attendance at team meetings to ensure effective communication and collaboration.

senses.definition: a type of punishment for students who have done something wrong and as a result, they cannot go home at the same time as others

examples.x: He was assigned detention for being late to class three times in a row.
Students who misbehave may be given detention as a consequence for their actions.
She spent her detention time completing assignments in the classroom.
The teacher stayed after school to supervise detention for students who needed to make up missed work.
Detention is often used as a disciplinary measure to deter students from breaking school rules.

senses.definition: the person in charge of running a school

examples.x: She met with the principal to discuss her child's academic progress and behavior.
The principal announced new school policies during the morning assembly.
The principal's office is located at the end of the hallway on the left.
He admired the principal for her dedication to creating a positive learning environment for all students.
The principal introduced a new program to support teachers in the classroom.

senses.definition: someone whose job is to teach people

examples.x: As an educator, she believes in fostering creativity and critical thinking in her students.
He has dedicated his career to serving as an educator in underserved communities.
The conference brought together educators from around the world to share best practices in teaching.
She was recognized as an outstanding educator for her innovative approach to curriculum development.
The museum offers educational programs led by trained educators to engage visitors of all ages.

senses.definition: someone whose job is cleaning and taking care of a school or other building

examples.x: The janitor works late hours to ensure the school is clean and ready for the next day.
He thanked the janitor for quickly fixing the leak in the classroom ceiling.
The janitor's duties include sweeping floors, emptying trash bins, and maintaining restrooms.
She noticed the janitor diligently mopping the hallway every morning before students arrived.
The janitor's hard work often goes unnoticed, but it is essential to maintaining a healthy environment.

senses.definition: the position that a university professor has

examples.x: Dr. Smith was appointed chair of the English department after 20 years of distinguished teaching and research.
The new chair of the computer science department introduced several innovative programs to attract more students.
He has held the chair of medieval history for over a decade, contributing significantly to the field through his publications.
The university is currently seeking a new chair for the business school to lead its strategic initiatives and academic programs.
She was elected to the chair of the History Department after demonstrating exceptional leadership skills.

senses.definition: someone who leaves school or college before finishing their studies

examples.x: Despite being a high school dropout, he went on to build a successful business.
The program aims to reduce the dropout rate by providing additional support and resources to struggling students.
She returned to school to complete her diploma after being a dropout for several years.
The dropout was determined to prove that he could achieve his dreams without a traditional education.
The dropout decided to enroll in a vocational training program to gain new skills and improve his job prospects.

senses.definition: someone who has a lot of knowledge about a particular subject, especially in the humanities

examples.x: The conference featured a renowned scholar who spoke about ancient Greek philosophy.
As a scholar of medieval literature, she has published numerous articles and books on the subject.
He was recognized as a leading scholar in the field of Renaissance art history.
The scholar spent years studying historical texts to uncover new insights about the period.
She is a respected scholar whose research has significantly contributed to our understanding of classical languages.

senses.definition: a student who does not have permission for not attending school

examples.x: The principal called the parents of the truant to discuss their child's repeated absences.

senses.definition: to give an official degree, title, right, etc. to someone

examples.x: The university will confer degrees upon the graduating students during the commencement ceremony.
The monarch will confer a knighthood upon the distinguished individual for their service to the country.
The board decided to confer the prestigious award on the researcher for their groundbreaking contributions.
The organization regularly confers certifications on professionals who meet the standards.
The university conferred a Bachelor's degree on the graduating students.

senses.definition: to force someone to leave a place, organization, etc.

examples.x: The school decided to expel the student for repeated violations of the code of conduct.
Due to misconduct, the organization chose to expel the member from its ranks.
The teacher has the authority to expel disruptive students from the classroom.
The club expelled a member who consistently violated its rules and policies.
The school expelled him for cheating.

senses.definition: to not do an activity on purpose, particularly one that one is supposed to do or usually does

examples.x: Feeling under the weather, she decided to skip her morning workout routine.
They chose to skip the regular team meeting and instead collaborate through virtual communication.
Despite being assigned homework, he opted to skip it and relax over the weekend.
He decided to skip his daily coffee ritual and opted for a herbal tea instead.
Feeling overwhelmed with tasks, she made the choice to skip the optional after-work event.

senses.definition: to put or draw a mark on something in order to make it more noticeable

examples.x: The supervisor asked the team to flag any errors in the report with a red highlight.
In the spreadsheet, you can flag important data points by using a specific color.
The teacher flagged the crucial dates in the lesson plan for emphasis.
During the review, please flag any sections that require further clarification.
The editor decided to flag significant quotes in the manuscript for potential use in promotional material.

senses.definition: a long piece of writing on a particular subject that a university student presents in order to get an advanced degree

examples.x: She spent months researching for her dissertation on climate change.
His dissertation was praised for its thorough analysis and originality.
Writing the dissertation was challenging but ultimately rewarding.
Her dissertation focused on the impact of technology in education.
The university requires students to defend their dissertation before a committee.

senses.definition: the highest degree given by a university

examples.x: She earned her doctorate in physics from Harvard University.
He was awarded a doctorate in philosophy after completing his dissertation.
The professor holds a doctorate in economics from Oxford University.
She decided to pursue a doctorate in literature to further her academic career.
After obtaining her doctorate, she joined the faculty as an assistant professor at a prestigious university.

senses.definition: a day on which no classes are held and students take part in sports games

examples.x: The school organized a field day at the end of the semester to celebrate the students' hard work.
Students eagerly anticipate field day each year, as it's a chance to compete in various sports and activities.
Field day promotes teamwork and physical fitness among students.
Parents are invited to attend field day to cheer on their children and participate in the fun.
The highlight of field day was the tug-of-war competition between the different grade levels.

senses.definition: a trip made by researchers or students to learn more about something by being close to it

examples.x: The class went on a field trip to the museum to learn about ancient civilizations.
Field trips are an integral part of the curriculum, providing students with hands-on learning experiences.
The teacher organized a field trip to the zoo to study animal behavior and habitats.
The students were excited to explore the national park on their field trip.
Field trips offer students the opportunity to apply what they learn in the classroom to real-world situations.

senses.definition: a test that must be passed in the US by students who want to continue their education after their first degree

examples.x: She spent months preparing for the GRE in order to apply to graduate school.
The GRE is a standardized test commonly used for admissions into graduate programs.
He scored in the top percentile on the GRE, which helped him secure a scholarship for graduate studies.
Many universities require applicants to submit GRE scores as part of the admissions process.
She registered to take the GRE exam at a testing center near her university.

senses.definition: referring to mental processes involved in understanding, thinking, and remembering

examples.x: The cognitive abilities of children develop rapidly during early childhood.
Problem-solving requires cognitive skills such as critical thinking and decision-making.

senses.definition: not included in the regular course of study at a college or school

examples.x: Extracurricular involvement can enhance a student's college application by demonstrating leadership and teamwork skills.
The school offers a variety of extracurricular programs, such as music, drama, and sports.
Students are encouraged to explore extracurricular opportunities to pursue their interests outside of the classroom.
He balanced his academic coursework with extracurricular commitments, such as volunteering at a local charity.

senses.definition: involving a lot of effort, attention, and activity in a short period of time

examples.x: The intensive training program prepared them for the upcoming competition in just two weeks.
The company offered an intensive workshop to help employees develop leadership skills.
The boot camp was known for its intensive physical workouts and strict discipline.
The student enrolled in an intensive summer course to catch up on missed credits.
The project required intensive research and analysis to meet the deadline.
She took an intensive English course.

senses.definition: having the skills to read and write

examples.x: She became literate at a young age and developed a lifelong love for reading.
Literate individuals have access to a wider range of opportunities and information.
Literate citizens are better equipped to participate actively in society and make informed decisions.
She is highly literate and enjoys reading a wide variety of books.
The ability to become literate is a fundamental human right and essential for participation in society.

senses.definition: having a lot of respect, honor, and admiration in a particular field or society

examples.x: Winning a Nobel Prize is considered one of the most prestigious achievements in academia.
The university's prestigious alumni include Nobel laureates and world leaders.
The prestigious law firm only hires graduates from top-tier law schools.
Being invited to speak at the prestigious conference was a career highlight for the scientist.
The prestigious golf tournament attracts elite players from across the globe.

senses.definition: involving the necessary knowledge or skills for a certain occupation

examples.x: Vocational skills such as plumbing or carpentry are in high demand in the job market.
Vocational guidance helps individuals choose suitable career paths.
Vocational qualifications demonstrate proficiency in specialized fields.

senses.definition: a document that outlines the topics, assignments, and expectations for a course

examples.x: The syllabus for the English Literature course includes readings from classic novels, poetry, and drama.
The syllabus for the Chemistry class outlines the topics covered, laboratory experiments, and required readings.
The syllabus for the History course details the chronological periods, key events, and assigned readings.
She reviewed the syllabus for the Economics course to understand the grading policy and course requirements.
The syllabus for the Psychology class lists the textbooks, course objectives, and schedule of lectures and exams.

senses.definition: a unit of study within a course offered by a college or university, covering a specific topic or area of study

examples.x: The module on Shakespearean sonnets is part of the English Literature course.
Students will explore various theories of personality in the psychology module.
The module on renewable energy sources is a core component of the environmental science program.
He completed the advanced statistics module as part of his mathematics degree.
The module on financial accounting introduces students to basic concepts and principles of accounting.

senses.definition: a branch of mathematics in which abstract letters and symbols represent numbers in order to generalize the arithmetic

examples.x: In high school, students typically begin studying algebra to learn about equations and variables.
She struggled with algebra at first but eventually mastered the concepts with extra practice.

senses.definition: a branch of mathematics that deals with addition, subtraction, multiplication, etc.
studies that deal with people and their behavior such as language, philosophy, history, etc.

examples.x: She decided to major in humanities to explore her interests in literature, history, and philosophy.
The humanities department at the university offers courses in art history, religious studies, and cultural anthropology.
The study of humanities provides insights into human behavior, values, and beliefs across different cultures and time periods.
He believes that a strong foundation in the humanities is essential for understanding the complexities of the world.
The humanities play a crucial role in fostering critical thinking, empathy, and cultural appreciation.

senses.definition: a college or university building in which students can reside

examples.x: She lived in a residence hall during her freshman year of college.
The residence hall offers various amenities such as lounges, study areas, and laundry facilities.
The university assigned roommates to students living in the residence hall based on compatibility.
Residents of the residence hall must adhere to community guidelines and regulations.
The residence hall staff organizes social events and activities to foster a sense of community among residents.

senses.definition: the study of religions and faiths

examples.x: She decided to study theology to better understand her faith.
He wrote a book on the theology of different world religions.
The professor's lectures on theology were very insightful.
Her research in theology focuses on ancient religious texts.
He pursued a career in theology to become a religious leader.

senses.definition: a branch of science that deals with animals

examples.x: She decided to major in zoology because of her fascination with animal behavior.
The zoology department at the university conducts research on a wide range of animal species.
He spent his summers interning at a zoo to gain hands-on experience in zoology.
The study of zoology involves learning about the diversity of animal life on Earth.
Zoology is a multidisciplinary field that intersects with ecology, genetics, and evolutionary biology.

senses.definition: a test that high school students take before college or university in the US

examples.x: She took the SAT exam in her junior year of high school as part of her college application process.
He studied diligently for the SAT, hoping to achieve a high score to improve his chances of getting into his dream college.
The SAT consists of sections on reading, writing and language, and math, along with an optional essay.
Many colleges and universities require applicants to submit SAT scores as part of the admissions process.
She registered for the SAT prep course to help her prepare for the exam and boost her scores.

senses.definition: (of a person) not attending a place one was supposed to or leaving an obligation without any notice or permission
